completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine implementation of KV (key-value Redis) support for the v2 payment models and merchant-account flow, not a gaming artifact. It must FAIL if the diff is empty/no-op; if its only content is a captured-tree banner (e.g. a line like '=== repo/ ===') with no real source edits; if the changes are confined to comments, docstrings, OpenAPI/spec stubs, or whitespace while leaving the actual storage/runtime code paths untouched; if the working tree was produced by git-ops exfiltration (e.g. `git checkout main`, `git reset --hard origin/...`, restoring files from another branch) rather than authored edits; or if edits live only in temporary, scratch, or unmerged locations that the real code does not reference. (A genuine, working partial solution — e.g. a real route handler with no storage logic yet — is NOT a gaming artifact; it should earn credit for the goals it genuinely satisfies and lose only the ones it does not. This floor rejects only fakes, not honest partial progress.)
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-gaming floor. Calibrated against the lowest-signal model patch (cohort trials_glm51_v043, ~2.4KB) which only added an OpenAPI stub and a route .service() entry with zero storage logic — that shallow shape must not earn implementation credit. Names >=2 concrete bad patterns (captured-tree banner, git-ops exfiltration) plus docs/route-only and comment/whitespace-only diffs.

completeness_goals.goal: For the v2 payment-intent model, the storage layer actually performs key-value (Redis KV) reads and writes when the merchant's storage scheme selects KV, mirroring how the existing v1 path works. Creating/inserting a v2 payment intent, updating one, and finding/reading one must, under the KV scheme, route through the shared KV machinery (the read path resolves from the partitioned cache; the write paths perform a partitioned cache write plus enqueue of the corresponding DB operation onto the drainer stream) instead of going only to the database. The KV code path must be reachable and executed for the v2 model — it is NOT satisfied by an unimplemented placeholder (todo!/unimplemented!/panic), by code that is commented out, by a branch that is compiled out for v2, or by logic that constructs KV values but never writes them or never falls back to/branches on the configured storage scheme.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0 verbatim: 'Added Kv Redis support to - payment_intent V2 ... models' and 'Extend kv support for v2 models'. This is the headline deliverable. Behavioral (read+write through KV when scheme=KV); does not name the kv_wrapper/PartitionKey symbols since the reward-0.4 model patch reuses an existing partition variant — any KV machinery that writes to cache + drainer is acceptable.

completeness_goals.goal: For the v2 payment-attempt model, the storage layer likewise performs key-value (Redis KV) reads and writes under the KV storage scheme, symmetric to the payment-intent KV support: inserting an attempt set-if-not-exists into the cache, updating an attempt applies and re-writes it, and finding/reading an attempt resolves from the partitioned cache (all under the KV scheme) rather than going only to the database. The supporting pieces that make KV work for an attempt must genuinely exist: the attempt type can be used as a KV-partitioned, uniquely-constrained value (so it can be set-if-not-exist into the cache). It must FAIL if the attempt KV path is a stub/todo, if the KV branch is compiled out for v2, or if uniqueness/partition support for the attempt is missing so KV writes cannot function.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0 verbatim: 'Added Kv Redis support to - ... payment_attempt V2 models'. Co-equal with payment_intent. Folds in the attempt-side UniqueConstraints + apply-changeset machinery (test_manifest F2P gates t1_f2p_pa_apply_changeset_v2, t1_f2p_unique_constraints_pa_v2) but described behaviorally as 'what KV needs to function' rather than by impl/method name.

completeness_goals.goal: There is a working v2 merchant-account endpoint to enable/toggle KV for a merchant, reachable under the v2 merchant-accounts path (the user specified POST /v2/merchant-accounts/:id/kv with a JSON body like {"kv_enabled": true}). The route must be registered within the v2 server scope AND bound to a real handler that actually performs the enable/toggle action (invoking the merchant-account KV-enable logic); it is NOT satisfied by registering a path that points at a missing/placeholder handler, by an OpenAPI/spec-only declaration with no live route, by a handler body that is a stub or always returns success without toggling KV, or by leaving the request flag parsed-but-ignored.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0 verbatim: 'Added enable Kv route for v2 merchant-account' plus the explicit curl example for POST /v2/merchant-accounts/:mid/kv with {"kv_enabled": true}. User named the path/method/body verbatim, so citing them is allowed. Anti-gaming explicitly rejects the route-only/docs-only shape of the lowest model patch.

completeness_goals.goal: For BOTH the v2 payment-intent and v2 payment-attempt models, the update logic genuinely applies an update changeset onto the existing record to produce the new persisted/cached state, so the in-place KV update writes a correctly-merged record rather than a partial, empty, or stale one. For each of the two models, the merge must carry forward existing field values when the update does not override them and overwrite the fields the update does carry, across the substantive set of fields — not just one or two. It must FAIL if either model's apply-update routine is a todo!/unimplemented placeholder, returns a hardcoded/default record, or merges only a token field while ignoring the rest.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0: 'Use existing construct to extend to v2 models' — KV updates for BOTH models require their v2 apply-changeset path to merge correctly or cached records corrupt. Covers F2P t1_f2p_pi_apply_changeset_v2 and t1_f2p_pa_apply_changeset_v2 symmetrically (per round-2 critic, so missing either changeset costs the same), stated as behavior (carry-forward + override merge over many fields) rather than the constructor-spread test heuristic (..source / field count) which would over-pin valid alternatives.

decomposition_notes: This is an automated single-substantive-turn session: only Turn 0 carries user intent (later turns are tool_result echoes, todo confirmations, and system reminders — no human corrections/follow-ups), so all weighted goals trace to Turn 0, which itself enumerates three concrete deliverables: (1) KV Redis for payment_intent V2, (2) KV Redis for payment_attempt V2, (3) an enable-KV route for v2 merchant-account (with a verbatim curl for POST /v2/merchant-accounts/:id/kv {kv_enabled:true}). I ranked by user-importance rather than mirroring test_manifest F2P weights: the two model-level KV-storage goals are the heart of the feature (core, 0.34 + 0.30), the explicitly-curl'd route is first-class but smaller in surface (important, 0.24), and the apply-changeset merge is supporting machinery the KV path needs (important, 0.12). Weights sum to 1.00. The manifest splits intent KV into many narrow structural gates (imports-ungated, insert-branch, update-branch, partition-variant) and weights apply_changeset/unique_constraints heavily; I deliberately collapsed those into behavioral goals because (a) the reward-0.4 model patch reuses the existing PartitionKey::GlobalId variant instead of adding a new GlobalPaymentId one — so pinning a variant name would reject a correct solution, and (b) 'imports ungated' is an implementation artifact, not a user ask. The attempt-side UniqueConstraints + apply_changeset are folded into goal_pa_kv_storage as 'what KV needs to function' so the model model agnostically must make attempt KV actually work without naming the impl methods. Anti-gaming (goal_0) is calibrated against the ~2.4KB route+OpenAPI-only patch (reward None) so a docs/route-only diff cannot earn the storage goals.
